behaviors.description: TSP heuristic produces routes within 1.5x optimal distance for inputs up to 20 stops
behaviors.justification: This is a pure algorithmic correctness test. Real failure: heuristic produces routes longer than 1.5x optimal. The test needs hardcoded coordinate sets with pre-computed optimal routes, real distance calculations (not mocked—we're testing the algorithm, not the distance function), and in-process computation. No external I/O, databases, or APIs needed. Distance calculation should use the real distance formula (Euclidean/Manhattan) but can use hardcoded test coordinates.
behaviors.test_description: Create 3–5 known test coordinate sets (5–20 points each) with pre-computed optimal or near-optimal routes. For each set: (1) run the heuristic, (2) compute actual route distance using the real distance function, (3) assert actual_distance ≤ 1.5 × optimal_distance. Include edge cases: collinear points, duplicate locations, widely spaced clusters.

behaviors.description: Delivery time window calculations use correct formula and apply configured road-type speeds
behaviors.justification: Real failure: formula is wrong (e.g., time = speed / distance instead of distance / speed) or configured speed values are not applied. The calculation is pure deterministic math: time = distance / speed. Test can parametrize distance and speed directly without loading from an external config source. No I/O, database queries, or API calls required—all values can be injected as test parameters.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrized unit test with (distance, speed, expected_time) tuples covering: normal cases (100 km at 80 km/h = 1.25 h), boundary cases (0 distance → 0 time), rounding and unit conversion edge cases, very high/low speeds. Assert calculation against expected values. Configuration loading (if applicable) can be tested separately at Integration level if needed.

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes exceeding remaining shift hours and returns excess amount
behaviors.justification: Pure validation logic with deterministic comparison and subtraction. Real failures: wrong operator (< vs ≤), inverted subtraction (hours - time vs time - hours), off-by-one errors, or edge cases (0 remaining, negative values). Create route and driver objects in-memory with various time/hours combinations. No external I/O, database, or API calls needed.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrized unit test with (route_estimated_time, driver_remaining_hours, should_reject, expected_excess): (8h, 10h, False, -2h), (12h, 10h, True, 2h), (0h, 10h, False, -10h), (10h, 0h, True, 10h), (10h, 10h, boundary_case, 0h). Verify rejection/acceptance decision and excess calculation accuracy for each case.

behaviors.description: Service skips addresses that fail geocoding (API returns no results) and reports which addresses were skipped
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on integrating with an external geocoding API. Real failure: service does not skip failed addresses, or fails to report them, or includes skipped addresses in optimization. Mocking the geocoding client is necessary (full System-level testing with the real API would be flaky and slow), but the mock must accurately represent the real API contract (response format, error codes). Test wires service + mocked geocoding client together (Integration), configures mock to return no results for specific addresses, and verifies skip and report logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: (1) Mock geocoding client to return valid results for 3 addresses but return empty/null for 1 address (matching the real API contract). (2) Call service with a list of 4 addresses. (3) Verify: optimization runs on 3 addresses only, skip report includes the 1 failed address with correct identifier, no optimization constraint references the skipped address. (4) Edge cases: all addresses fail, none fail, first/last fail. Ensure mock response format matches real API (e.g., {results: []} not None).

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice
behaviors.justification: Pure data deduplication and consolidation logic. Real failures: duplicates not detected (address comparison), items not combined, or duplicate address still appears in the final route. Test creates in-memory address lists with duplicates and associated delivery items, runs deduplication, and verifies the output contains each address only once with all items combined. No I/O, database, or external calls needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with parametrized inputs: (1) [addr_A[item1], addr_A[item2], addr_B[item3]] → {addr_A: [item1, item2], addr_B: [item3]}. (2) Case-insensitive and whitespace-insensitive address matching (e.g., '123 Main St' == '123 MAIN ST'). (3) Verify route optimization does not visit the address twice. (4) Edge cases: all duplicates, no duplicates, address with empty item list, numeric vs. string address formats.
